reasons: Khufra directly punishes Nolan's dash-based entry and escape pattern.
Nolan needs clean movement paths to connect rifts, while Khufra can interrupt that rhythm.

proof.summary: Nolan relies on dash movement to create and trigger rifts, while Khufra's anti-blink control can punish Nolan when he tries to cross through the fight.
worksBestWhen: Khufra saves Bouncing Ball for Nolan's real engage.
Khufra positions between Nolan and Nolan's intended target.
The protected carry stays close enough for Khufra to peel.
failureCases: Nolan baits Khufra's anti-dash before committing.
Khufra is forced to engage first and loses peel position.
Nolan enters from an angle Khufra cannot cover.

reasons: Minsitthar's ultimate field can deny directional blink skills, which directly restricts Nolan's mobility inside the fight.
Nolan becomes much easier to punish when he cannot freely dash through his rift pattern.

proof.summary: Nolan wants to move through targets with repeated dashes, while Minsitthar can create a zone where directional blink skills are blocked.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar places King's Calling around Nolan's target or escape route.
The team follows up before Nolan exits the field.
Minsitthar waits for Nolan to commit instead of casting too early.
failureCases: Nolan waits outside the zone and enters after it expires.
Minsitthar uses ultimate too early on a low-value target.
Nolan attacks a different backline angle outside the field.

reasons: Franco gives a simple answer to Nolan's mobility by catching him with suppression before or after his dash chain.
Nolan's durability is not the main threat; his movement uptime is, so targeted lock is valuable.

proof.summary: Nolan wants fast in-out burst, while Franco can suppress him during a punish window and stop the escape pattern long enough for follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Franco waits for Nolan to spend a dash.
Franco controls fog paths that Nolan wants to use.
failureCases: Franco misses hook and Nolan gets free backline access.
Nolan enters from fog after Franco has used suppression.
Franco locks Nolan without enough allied damage to finish him.

reasons: Kaja can punish Nolan with targeted suppression and drag him away from his intended burst route.
This is reliable when Kaja waits for Nolan's entry instead of chasing him blindly.

proof.summary: Nolan wants to cross into the backline and leave, while Kaja can lock and reposition him during the dive window.
worksBestWhen: Kaja saves Divine Judgment for Nolan's committed engage.
The team bursts Nolan during suppression.
Kaja stands near Nolan's likely target.
failureCases: Kaja gets poked out before Nolan commits.
Nolan avoids Kaja's range and attacks a different lane angle.
Kaja uses suppression before Nolan spends mobility.

reasons: Saber can punish Nolan when Nolan has spent mobility tools and has no clean escape route.
The matchup depends on timing because Nolan can dodge or disengage if Saber commits too early.

proof.summary: Nolan is dangerous while his dash chain is available, but Saber can punish him after a mobility commit with single-target airborne burst.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits for Nolan to use a mobility skill.
Saber has enough burst damage to finish the punish window.
failureCases: Saber ults while Nolan still has escape tools.
Nolan invades Saber before Saber reaches burst timing.
Nolan's team peels during Saber's commit.
